steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that Chekhov portrays Professor Serebrakoff as "unable to adjust to living at the country estate." The correct quotation must show the professor himself expressing that he cannot adapt — not just general incompetence, conflict with others, or criticism from someone else.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Professor Serebrakoff says, "I am a book-worm and a scholar, and am unfamiliar with practical affairs..."': shows he lacks practical skills, but not that he struggles to adjust to the estate specifically. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Professor Serebrakoff says to Helena, "My darling, don't leave me alone with [Vanya]..."': reveals anxiety about Vanya, not about country life. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Professor Serebrakoff says to Helena, "I am used to my library and the lecture hall...Now I suddenly find myself plunged into this wilderness, condemned to see the same stupid people..."': the professor directly contrasts his former academic life with the estate, using "plunged into" and "condemned to" to express his failure to adjust. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Vanya says to Professor Serebrakoff, "You write on art without knowing anything about it..."': this is Vanya criticizing the professor, not the professor's own view of the estate. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
